evaluator: fresh independent full-epic Level 2 evaluator (after all slices merge)
approval_notes: Orchestrator-directed F005 Slice 4 (FINAL F005 slice). Build ONE slice, open ONE PR, do NOT merge, do NOT flip any passes flag. F005 stays passes:false; a separate fresh-context full-epic Level 2 close-out owns the flip after this slice merges.

Builds on Slice 1 (merged 405264a: Cloudflare official extraction + config->DB sync + runner + migration 0007), Slice 2 (merged d377d0a: deterministic gated publication path), and Slice 3 (merged 1b7fb34: read-only /catalogue HTTP API). This slice CONSUMES the S3 read API over the nginx /api proxy and renders it as a public web experience.
Owner-confirmed (Q2): a SINGLE Cloudflare-focused provider page. Catalogue-wide search/compare + the adviser are deferred to F006 and are OUT OF SCOPE here.

Standing autonomy boundaries: may branch/implement/test/commit/push/open ONE PR; may NOT merge, flip any passes flag, add a Python/npm runtime dependency, add a migration, weaken any existing test/fixture/CHECK/trigger, touch trg_offer_version_immutable or the two 0006 separation triggers, add a backend endpoint or backend mutation, access the DB directly from the web, or add an LLM/network/user-controlled-URL path.
objective: Deliver a genuinely working PUBLIC web experience (apps/web) that consumes ONLY the S3 read-only catalogue HTTP API over the same-origin /api proxy and renders the real published Cloudflare data end-to-end. A single Cloudflare-focused provider page rendering: (1) category/service states with clear Z0 badges; (2) each published offer's Z0 zero_cost_class as a plain-language badge + the human-readable WHY (material_facts.classification.reasons surfaced by the API) in plain language; (3) the official evidence backing an offer (source + provenance + link to the official page, clearly official-evidence-backed); (4) the plain-language confidence LABEL as the primary signal, with the numeric score/signals only in an advanced/details disclosure (D039); (5) the offer's version history + change events; (6) completeness + freshness signals; (7) the offer's quota rows (amount/unit/reset period/exhaustion behaviour) readably. Honest 'Unknown' when the API returns null/unknown; never fabricated. Accessibility is part of done.
scope: apps/web/src/api.ts: extend the existing minimal client with read-only TS types + GET fetchers for the S3 catalogue endpoints (provider detail, category-states, provider offers, offer detail, offer evidence, offer history). Same-origin /api prefix (nginx proxy / vite dev proxy); credential-free, actionable error messages; AbortSignal support. No hard-coded host, no CORS, no user-supplied URL.

apps/web/src/catalogue/ (new): presentational React components for the Cloudflare provider page - ProviderHeader (name/type/completeness/freshness/official domains + counts), Z0Badge (Z0 class -> plain-language label paired with a text/icon token, never color-only), ConfidenceLabel (plain-language label as primary; numeric only inside an advanced <details> block), CategoryStates (categories -> services -> offers with Z0 + confidence), OfferCard (Z0 badge + plain-language why/reasons + blocking conditions + quotas table + confidence label + completeness/freshness + advanced disclosure), EvidenceList (official source + provenance + official link), OfferHistory (version list + change events). Semantic HTML, keyboard-navigable, labelled regions, honest 'Unknown' for null values.

apps/web/src/App.tsx: replace the F002 health-check landing with the Cloudflare provider experience - loads provider detail + category-states + offers, then per offer its detail/evidence/history from the read API, and renders scope items 1-7. Loading, error (with retry), and empty/degraded states are all handled honestly. Keeps the API same-origin via /api.
apps/web/src/App.css: styling for the provider page + badges (color paired with text; sufficient contrast; responsive). No new dependency; plain CSS only.

apps/web/src/api.test.ts (new) + apps/web/src/App.test.tsx (updated) + apps/web/src/catalogue/*.test.tsx (new): offline, deterministic vitest + Testing Library tests with mocked fetch covering each scope item (1-7), the confidence LABEL-is-primary / numeric-only-in-advanced rule, honest 'Unknown' rendering when a field is null, basic accessibility assertions (headings/roles/labels, badges are not color-only), and the credential-free error + retry path.
apps/web/README.md: update to describe the Cloudflare provider experience and the catalogue endpoints it consumes (replacing the 'scaffold only' note for this page).
docs/ARCHITECTURE.md: document the web experience consuming the read-only catalogue API over /api (read-only, no direct DB, no new backend endpoint). Append an agent-state/progress.md handoff.
out_of_scope: Any new backend endpoint or backend mutation; any direct database access from the web (the web consumes only the S3 read API over /api).

Any new Python or npm RUNTIME dependency; no pyproject/requirements*/package.json runtime dependency change (requirements-sync + npm audit stay green; reuse React 19 + the existing vitest/testing-library tooling). If a hard blocker requires a new dependency, STOP and report.
Any database migration or change to publish/classify/ingest semantics; weakening OfferVersion immutability (trg_offer_version_immutable) or the two 0006 separation triggers.
Catalogue-wide search / cross-provider compare / the architecture adviser / 'chat with your data' (deferred to F006).
Any user-controlled URL / fetchable host from the UI (no SSRF surface); the page only calls fixed same-origin /api/catalogue paths with internal identifiers.
Flipping F005 passes:true or editing any feature_list.json passes flag (reserved for the post-merge full-epic Level 2 close-out).
acceptance_criteria: READ-ONLY consumption: the web calls only GET /api/catalogue/* endpoints; no write/mutation; no new backend endpoint; no direct DB access from the web.
Category/service states: the page renders Cloudflare's services grouped by category, each published offer with a clear Z0 badge (color paired with text/icon, never color-only).
Offers + plain-language why: each published offer shows its Z0 zero_cost_class as a plain-language badge and the human-readable reasons (why it is / isn't Z0), plus blocking conditions when present.
Evidence: the official evidence backing an offer is shown with source + provenance and a link to the official page, and is clearly marked official-evidence-backed.
Confidence: the plain-language confidence LABEL is the primary signal; the numeric score/signals appear only inside an advanced/details disclosure (D039).
History: the offer's version history + change events (what changed and when) are rendered.
Completeness & freshness: these signals are surfaced per offer and per provider so the user can judge data completeness/freshness.
Quotas: each offer's quota rows (amount/unit/reset period/exhaustion behaviour) are rendered readably.
'unknown is better than guessed': a null/unknown value from the API is shown honestly (e.g. 'Unknown'), never fabricated.
Accessibility: semantic HTML, keyboard navigability, labelled regions/roles, no color-only Z0 signaling, sufficient contrast; basic a11y assertions are covered by tests.
No new dependency, no migration; requirements-sync + npm audit stay green; immutability + separation triggers intact; publish/classify/ingest semantics untouched; no user-controlled URL.

Regression green offline: scripts/test.ps1 (pytest + web vitest + web BUILD) and scripts/check.ps1 -NodeAudit (ruff lint+format, pytest, prettier, eslint, detect-secrets, pip-audit, npm audit, requirements-sync) pass.

Live stack proves the page renders REAL published Cloudflare data end-to-end (category states + Z0 badges, offers with plain-language why, evidence + official link, confidence label with numeric only in advanced, history + change events, completeness/freshness, quotas).
F005 remains passes:false; a fresh-context independent full-epic Level 2 evaluator (post all-slices merge) owns any status change.

integration_commands: Fresh live stack on alternate host ports (POSTGRES_PORT=55432, API_PORT=8010, WEB_PORT=8090, DATABASE_URL=postgresql+psycopg://atlas:atlas@localhost:55432/atlas): docker compose down --volumes -> scripts/stack-up.ps1 --build (fresh DB applies 0001-0007) -> run S1 scan + S2 publish (python -m app.ingest.runner --publish against the captured Cloudflare fixtures) so the DB has published Cloudflare offers -> scripts/stack-smoke.ps1 -> load the web experience over WEB_PORT=8090 and verify it renders the REAL published data end-to-end (Cloudflare category states + Z0 badges, offers with plain-language why, evidence + official link, confidence LABEL with numeric only in advanced, history + change events, completeness/freshness, quotas); capture rendered evidence (served bundle + the /api/catalogue responses the page consumes, and/or a headless render check); confirm honest degradation when a field is unknown -> docker compose down --volumes.

risks.data_migration: None: Slice 4 adds NO migration. It is a frontend-only slice consuming the existing read API. Migration head stays 0007.
risks.security_privacy: Web consumes only fixed same-origin /api/catalogue GET paths with internal identifiers; no user-controlled URL (no SSRF surface); no direct DB access; no new backend endpoint; no LLM; no secrets. Only published catalogue data (as the S3 API exposes it) is rendered; community/candidate data is never fetched.
risks.portability: Reuses React 19 + the existing vite/vitest/testing-library tooling already in apps/web; no new runtime dependency; amd64/arm64 unaffected.
risks.zero_cost_safety: The UI renders the Z0 class, reasons, and confidence exactly as the API returns them; it never re-derives or upgrades a class and never fabricates a value. Unknown/absent values render as an honest 'Unknown'.
risks.immutability: Frontend-only: no INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE anywhere. trg_offer_version_immutable and the two 0006 separation triggers are untouched.
risks.regression: Replaces the F002 health-check landing page (its tests are updated accordingly) and adds new components/tests. The API, worker, scheduler, nginx proxy, and stack are unchanged; /health* and container health endpoints preserved.
risks.supply_chain: No new Python or npm runtime dependency; requirements-sync + npm audit stay green; stdlib + already-present deps only.

notes: Orchestrator directed F005 Slice 4 (final slice): the public Cloudflare provider web experience consuming the S3 read API over /api, then STOP for a fresh-context full-epic Level 2 close-out. Do NOT flip passes; do NOT merge; do NOT add a migration or a dependency; consume only the read API (no direct DB, no new backend endpoint); no user-controlled URLs; accessibility is part of done; honest unknowns; do NOT weaken immutability/separation.
